title: submission: Three-Layer Governance Probe + Cheap Gate v2 (Signs-of-Life)
author: SyntagmaNull

data.description: Three-layer governance supervision chain with Cael generation, Monday intervention policy, and Seryn audit layer.
Inference-time cheap gate policy that redirects on persistent-error and mark-only on pre-error/light-anomaly cases.
Minimal real_cael checkpoint adapter/export used to run the governance probe on a small model.
Training-side auxiliary supervision probes for zone classification and decision-shape prediction.

novel_contributions: Three-layer governance supervision chain (Cael -> Monday -> Seryn) wired to a minimal real_cael checkpoint
Readable two-family decision geometry separating persistent-error from pre-error regimes
Inference-time cheap gate v2 candidate that redirects on persistent-error and mark-only on pre-error
Local probe evidence that the gate removes overgovernance cost in the pre-error zone while preserving redirect gains in persistent-error cases
Training-side auxiliary supervision probes for zone classification and decision-shape prediction
Non-record signs-of-life submission documenting a pre-compute-grant mechanism rather than a leaderboard run
